title: Contribution #5 — imaging/pathology tool tutorial (open imaging)
context: Digital pathology and medical imaging tools for cancer research (cell segmentation, radiomics, nucleus detection) are among the most under-documented in the space. Imaging tutorials are riskTier medium because: (1) imaging data requires careful license and de-identification verification even for open tiers, and (2) any biological/clinical claim about what an image feature means requires domain-expert review. Data must be open imaging only — open-tier TCGA/CPTAC imaging or the tool's shipped sample images — with no controlled-access slides. Depends on harness-014 (imaging ecosystem support) and triage-009 (gate-passed imaging tool).
objective: Author and contribute a merged imaging/pathology tool tutorial using open imaging data only, following the Domain-reviewer path, with the data's license and de-identification verified in the gate artifact, biological claims cited, and the tutorial running reproducibly in CI.
acceptanceCriteria: Uses open imaging only: open-tier TCGA/CPTAC imaging or the tool's own shipped sample images; license and de-identification verified in the committed gate artifact; no controlled-access slides.
Target tool has a PASS gate artifact confirming OSI-permissive or copyleft license, acceptable contribution policy, and cancer-data guardrail clearance for the chosen imaging data.
Issue-first alignment confirmed with the maintainer before authoring.
Every biological or clinical assertion carries an inline citation; Domain reviewer (bioinformatician; oncologist where clinical context appears) sign-off obtained.
Mandatory 'research/education use — not medical advice' disclaimer present and prominent.
Tutorial runs reproducibly in CI from the pinned environment using the generalized harness (harness-014); reproEnv.ciRunRef recorded.
License + Data-provenance reviewer sign-off and Technical reviewer sign-off obtained.
Contribution merged upstream; outcome artifact committed at outcomes/onco-research-software-docs-contrib-015.json with merge-commit URL and coverage before/after.
resources: PLAN.md — Axis 2 cancer data (open-tier TCGA/CPTAC imaging)
Open-tier GDC/TCGA imaging access documentation; CPTAC open imaging
output: An imaging/pathology tutorial merged upstream for a gate-passed tool, using open imaging data only, with Domain reviewer sign-off, reproducible CI run, and outcome artifact recorded — extending project coverage into the digital-pathology ecosystem.
